title: Dog Coughing Fits at Night: Causes, Urgency, and When to See a Vet
meta_desc: Is your dog coughing at night? Learn about common causes, the urgency of care, and why video evidence is vital for a veterinary diagnosis.
content_html:
TL;DR: Nighttime dog coughing often signals heart or respiratory issues and typically requires a vet visit within 24 to 48 hours, unless your dog shows emergency signs like blue gums or severe breathing distress.

What causes my dog to cough more at night?

  • Nighttime coughing involves persistent respiratory irritation or distress that occurs specifically while your dog is resting.
  • The cough may sound like honking, hacking, or wet retching.
  • Lying down changes how fluids and organs sit, making issues with the heart or airway more pronounced.
  • Common underlying causes include kennel cough, tracheal collapse, or early signs of congestive heart failure.

Is my dog's nighttime coughing a medical emergency?

  • Most nighttime coughs are a medium priority and should be evaluated by a vet within 24 to 48 hours.
  • Seek immediate emergency care if the cough is accompanied by blue-tinged gums or extreme lethargy.
  • Go to the ER immediately if your dog is experiencing a total inability to catch their breath.

Why should I record a video of my dog coughing for the vet?

  • Dogs often stop coughing during exams because they are upright and excited in the clinic.
  • A home video captures the dog's natural posture and the specific sound of the cough.
  • Clear footage helps your veterinarian quickly differentiate between a collapsing trachea and fluid in the lungs.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Coughing fits, particularly severe after rest, a change of environment, or at the beginning of exercise, are a prominent sign of respiratory disease in dogs. While respiratory sounds may be normal on auscultation, inspiratory crackles and expiratory wheezes can be heard in advanced cases. Tracheobronchitis should be suspected when a characteristic cough develops 5-10 days after exposure to other dogs. Thoracic radiographs are essential to determine the severity of the disease and to exclude other causes of the cough. Development of more severe signs, including fever, purulent nasal discharge, depression, anorexia, and a productive cough, especially in puppies, indicates a complicating systemic infection such as distemper or bronchopneumonia.

Chapter: Infectious Disease, Respiratory

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1492)

faqs.question: Why does my dog only cough when lying down?
faqs.answer: Lying down can put pressure on the airway or allow fluid to accumulate in certain areas of the lungs, making respiratory or cardiac issues more apparent than when the dog is standing or active.

faqs.question: Can I give my dog human cough suppressants?
faqs.answer: No, you should never administer human medications to your dog without professional advice. Many human syrups contain xylitol or other ingredients that are highly toxic to pets.

faqs.question: Is a 'honking' cough different from a 'wet' cough?
faqs.answer: Yes. A dry, honking cough often suggests a structural issue like tracheal collapse or kennel cough, while a wet, productive cough often points toward fluid buildup or infection like pneumonia.
